title: usysparm
text: usysparm Call a ProcScript function and return its value. Explain this code long usysparm (shortcode, unsigned char*buffer, shortdummy); Copy Parameters code Numeric code identifying the ProcScript function. See the table below. buffer Parameter for the ProcScript function, if it has one. On return, buffer contains the string return value of the ProcScript function, if this is a ProcScript function that returns a string value (see the table below). dummy Obsolete and not used. Always supply the value zero. Return Values Table 1. Values returned by usysparm Value Meaning >0 The return value of the numeric ProcScript function 0 For a numeric function, the return value of the function. For a string function, zero indicates success; the value returned by the function is in Buffer . -1 An error occurred. Common causes depend on the function code used: 1001-2000 No current component 2001-3000 No current component, or entity not found in current component 3001-3999 No current component, or field not found in current component Use Library: yrtl Description usysparm calls the ProcScript function requested by code and returns its value, either in the return value (for numeric or boolean functions), or in buffer (for string functions). On calling, buffer can contain the additional parameter for the ProcScript function, if it has any (typically a field or entity name). It is important that you always initialize this buffer before calling. If you do not want to pass a specific value, set it to the empty string. On return, buffer will contain the result of the ProcScript function, if this function is a string function, and a zero return code if it was successfully called. The following routine can be used to append an asterisk to the component title when a modification is made in the component, or to remove this asterisk when the modification status is reset. This is similar to what many Windows editing-type programs do. The code uses usysparm to query the component title, and usetparm to change it if necessary. Explain this code #include <h3gl.h> EXPORT void SetModified (int modified) { char title[200]; int len, hasstar; *title = 0; usysparm(1006,title,0); /* Get component title */ len = strlen(title); hasstar = ( len > 0 && title[len-1] == '*' ); if ( modified ) { if ( ! hasstar ) { title[len] = '*'; title[len+1] = 0; usetparm(1006,title,0); /* Set new component title */ } } else { if ( hasstar ) { title[len-1] = 0; usetparm(1006,title,0); /* Set new component title */ } } } Copy Related Links ProcScript: Functions Refer to the topics under this section to understand functions related to ProcScript. usetparm Set the value of a ProcScript function.
